title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: May 20, 2026: Market rallies on strong earnings, Fed focus, and geopolitical tensions

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Tick Higher as Earnings Boost Sentiment, Nvidia in Focus
items.summary: U.S. equities are largely in the green today, buoyed by strong corporate earnings reports from major retailers and a slight easing in bond market pressures. The market is closely watching Nvidia's Q1 results, due after the bell, which are expected to provide further insights into the AI sector's health. Geopolitical tensions surrounding the Iran war continue to be a backdrop, influencing oil prices and bond yields.

items.headline: Target (TGT)
items.summary: Shares rose after the company reported stronger-than-expected first-quarter earnings and revenue, and subsequently raised its full-year revenue outlook.

items.headline: TJX Cos. (TJX)
items.summary: The retailer saw its stock climb after delivering stronger profit and revenue for the latest quarter than analysts had anticipated.

items.headline: Cava Group (CAVA)
items.summary: Rallied significantly following better-than-expected profit reports.

items.headline: Intel (INTC)
items.summary: Experienced notable gains among liquid option names.

items.headline: Nvidia (NVDA)
items.summary: Closed lower ahead of its highly anticipated first-quarter earnings report, with the broader tech sector facing pressure from rising bond yields.

items.headline: Nvidia Earnings Eyed Post-Market Close
items.summary: All eyes are on Nvidia's Q1 FY2027 earnings report, scheduled after today's market close. Analysts anticipate earnings per share around $1.75-$1.78 on revenue ranging from $70 billion to $79.2 billion, with expectations high for continued AI-driven growth.

items.headline: Retailers Report Strong Q1 Results: Target and Lowe's Exceed Expectations
items.summary: Target announced Q1 GAAP and Adjusted EPS of $1.71, surpassing estimates of $1.46, and raised its full-year revenue outlook. Lowe's also reported strong first-quarter results, with adjusted diluted EPS of $3.03 and total sales of $23.1 billion, both exceeding analyst forecasts.

items.headline: Analog Devices Posts Record Fiscal Q2 2026 Results
items.summary: Global semiconductor leader Analog Devices ($ADI) reported record financial results for its fiscal second quarter 2026, with revenue of $3.62 billion, marking a 37% year-over-year growth across all end markets.

items.headline: Treasury Yields Stabilize Amid Geopolitical Concerns
items.summary: Treasury yields eased slightly today, with the 10-year Treasury yield moving to 4.65% from 4.67% yesterday. However, yields remain elevated overall due to ongoing inflation concerns and geopolitical tensions stemming from the Iran war.

items.headline: FOMC Minutes and Macro Trends on Investor Radar
items.summary: Investors are anticipating the release of the April FOMC meeting minutes, which could provide further insights into the Federal Reserve's monetary policy stance. Broader macro trends, including the impact of the Iran war on oil prices (which fell today), and shifts in bond yields continue to shape market sentiment.
